newsSince: No bottleneck-changing news since June 9. May revenue NT$12.5B (double-digit YoY) and analyst price-target bumps (to ~NT$600) are incremental; OpenTitan root-of-trust membership is strategic but not a near-term driver. The +32.6% spike to all-time high is flow/revenue-print driven, not a new bottleneck position.

verdicts.rankRationale: On node-criticality, Realtek remains analog/RF/edge, not HBM/packaging/AI-networking core. The +32.6% surge since June 9 is a revenue-print/flow move (May rev +double-digit, PT bumps), not a new bottleneck position, further eroding residual alpha and confirming the prior de-rank.
verdicts.bull: AI/automotive Ethernet + Wi-Fi 7 growth; OpenTitan RoT optionality for datacenter security.
verdicts.neutral: Solid franchise but indirect to the 2027 compute bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Low directness vs HBM/packaging/AI-networking; +77% YTD on PC/networking-cycle beta with no choke-point.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Automotive Ethernet + AI edge revenue ramp.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO lens Realtek has weak direct CPO/HBM/packaging/test relevance; prior verdict was removal. The post-June-9 spike (revenue-driven) and PT hikes worsen the asymmetry without adding any CPO-relevant catalyst. De-rank stands.
verdicts.bull: Networking chip exposure; broad connectivity portfolio.
verdicts.neutral: Not a CPO bottleneck; edge networking is tangential.
verdicts.bear: No CPO directness; valuation re-rated on flow, not optical-interconnect alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mispriced as a CPO play; cleaner bottleneck names dominate.
